title: hypericum quadrangulum subsp. tetrapterum
intro: Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. tetrapterum, also known as cross-leaved St. John's Wort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the St. John's Wort genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. tetrapterum include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
